Functions for sending link status messages and handling unknown protocols.

# Purpose
This C header file declares three functions related to handling link layer communication events. The function [`pulse_llc_send_link_opened_msg`](<#pulse_llc_send_link_opened_msg>) is used to send a message indicating that a link has been opened. The function [`pulse_llc_send_link_closed_msg`](<#pulse_llc_send_link_closed_msg>) sends a message indicating that a link has been closed. The function [`pulse_llc_unknown_protocol_handler`](<#pulse_llc_unknown_protocol_handler>) handles packets associated with an unknown protocol, taking a `uint8_t` protocol identifier, a pointer to the packet data, and the size of the packet as parameters. The file includes the standard library header `<stdint.h>` for fixed-width integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### pulse\_llc\_send\_link\_opened\_msg<!-- {{#callable_declaration:pulse_llc_send_link_opened_msg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/pulse_llc.h#L21>)

Sends a link opened message using the LLC protocol.
- **Description**: Use this function to notify that a link has been opened by sending a message through the LLC protocol. This function does not take any parameters and does not return a value. It is intended to be called when a link is successfully established and a notification needs to be sent. Ensure that the necessary protocol and communication infrastructure are initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`pulse_llc_send_link_opened_msg`](<pulse_llc.c.md#pulse_llc_send_link_opened_msg>)  (Implementation)


---
### pulse\_llc\_send\_link\_closed\_msg<!-- {{#callable_declaration:pulse_llc_send_link_closed_msg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/pulse_llc.h#L23>)

Sends a link closed message using the LLC protocol.
- **Description**: Use this function to notify that a link has been closed by sending a message through the LLC protocol. This function does not take any parameters and does not return any value. It is intended to be called when a link closure needs to be communicated. Ensure that the LLC protocol is initialized and ready to send messages before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`pulse_llc_send_link_closed_msg`](<pulse_llc.c.md#pulse_llc_send_link_closed_msg>)  (Implementation)


---
### pulse\_llc\_unknown\_protocol\_handler<!-- {{#callable_declaration:pulse_llc_unknown_protocol_handler}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/pulse_llc.h#L25>)

Handles packets with unknown protocol numbers.
- **Description**: Use this function to process packets that have an unrecognized protocol number. It is important to call this function when a packet with an unknown protocol is received to ensure proper handling and response. This function does not modify the packet or the protocol number, but it triggers a response to indicate the unknown protocol condition.
- **Inputs**:
    - `protocol`: The protocol number of the packet. It is an 8-bit unsigned integer. The function does not validate this parameter, so it can be any value.
    - `packet`: A pointer to the packet data. The caller must ensure this pointer is valid and points to a memory area of at least 'length' bytes. The function does not modify the packet data.
    - `length`: The size of the packet data in bytes. It is a size_t value, and the caller must ensure it accurately represents the size of the data pointed to by 'packet'.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`pulse_llc_unknown_protocol_handler`](<pulse_llc.c.md#pulse_llc_unknown_protocol_handler>)  (Implementation)
